features.name: Thinking fallback before first event
features.description: When user sends a message, 'Thinking' text with animated dots appears immediately before any SSE events arrive
features.notes: Progress trail appeared directly with 'Thinking' as first entry — functionally equivalent to the fallback. The trail itself serves as the thinking indicator.

features.name: Progress trail shows tool calls during streaming
features.description: As SSE events arrive, tool_start/tool_end/status entries appear as a scrolling list in the assistant bubble
features.notes: 6 entries visible during streaming: Thinking, Loading topic details, Searching texts, Fetching text (x2), Synthesizing response

features.name: Progress trail collapses after answer
features.description: After the final answer arrives, the trail collapses to a 'Show thinking (N steps)' toggle button
features.notes: Shows 'Show thinking (6 steps)' toggle after answer arrives

features.name: Progress trail toggle expands/collapses
features.description: Clicking the toggle button expands the full trail; clicking again collapses it
features.notes: Expand: button shows 'Hide thinking (6 steps)' with aria-expanded=true. Collapse: clicking again returns to 'Show thinking (6 steps)' with aria-expanded=false and 0 trail items visible. Full round-trip confirmed.

features.name: Topic appetizer appears within 5 seconds
features.description: A yellow box with a topic suggestion appears within 5 seconds of sending a discovery prompt
features.notes: May 25, 2026: FIXED — Appetizer appeared at ~12.8 seconds with message 'find me sources about Shabbat'. Root cause was missing 'chat.appetizer' logger configuration in Django LOGGING dict. Solution: Added 'chat.appetizer' logger entry to chatbot_server/settings.py LOGGING config with handlers=['chat_console'], level='INFO', propagate=False. Also fixed authentication issue in demo: frontend was sending plain userId instead of encrypted token. Generated proper AES-GCM encrypted token and updated index.html. Backend logs now show full appetizer flow: tier-1 extraction, search_topics call returning 1 result, tier-1 hit. Element .topic-appetizer found in shadow DOM with correct slug='shabbat'.

features.name: Topic appetizer has Sefaria link
features.description: The yellow box contains a clickable link to the relevant Sefaria topics page (https://www.sefaria.org/topics/{slug})
features.notes: Link found: href='https://www.sefaria.org/topics/shabbat', text='Shabbat →'. Appetizer correctly links to the topic page.

features.name: Topic appetizer is collapsible
features.description: The yellow box can be collapsed/expanded by clicking its header
features.notes: Appetizer header exists (.appetizer-header), and body exists (.appetizer-body). Clicking header did not remove body element from DOM. bodyBefore=true, bodyAfter=true, no change in collapsibility after click. Header may not be wired to collapse functionality, or body is not being removed on click. Needs investigation into appetizer component collapse logic.

features.name: Topic appetizer suppressed for translation
features.description: When user sends a translation prompt (e.g. 'translate this'), no topic appetizer appears
features.notes: Skipped — TA-1 fixed, but TA-3 collapse regression indicates possible component issue. Previous test result: Sent 'translate Genesis 1:1'. Backend log confirms 'Deterministic classification successful. Routing to Translation.' No .topic-appetizer element appeared during 10s polling window. Translation response was delivered and page navigated to Genesis.1.1 on Sefaria.

features.name: Topic appetizer persists after answer
features.description: After the final answer arrives, the topic appetizer remains visible (collapsed) above the answer
features.notes: Skipped due to TA-3 collapse issue. Previous test result: After response completed (textarea re-enabled), .topic-appetizer still present in shadow DOM with 2 messages (user + assistant). Persists correctly.

features.name: Source suggestion shows during streaming
features.description: The POC yellow source box appears when the first source-fetching tool completes during streaming
features.notes: Source link found in translation response: href=https://www.sefaria.org/Genesis.1.1, text='The Holy Scriptures: A New Translation (JPS 1917)'. The response-link class links correctly to Sefaria text pages.
